name: Port Augusta Renewable Energy Park - Wind
notable: 50x Vestas V150-4.2MW (wind) + 110MW solar. BHP Olympic Dam PPA + Woolworths PPA. Owned by Iberdrola. Elecnor EPC.

timeline.title: Financial close
timeline.detail: DP Energy / Iberdrola reached financial close

timeline.title: Construction commenced
timeline.detail: Site works began for 50 Vestas turbines near Port Augusta

timeline.title: Commercial operations commenced
timeline.detail: Commencement date from AEMO registration via OpenElectricity.

timeline.title: First generation recorded
timeline.detail: First market dispatch data recorded in AEMO systems.

eis_specs.document_title: Port Augusta Renewable Energy Park Development Application (SA EPA / EPBC 2018/8280)
eis_specs.hub_height_note: Standard hub height for V150-4.2 on SA coastal plain
eis_specs.wind_speed_period: Mean annual wind speed at hub height; Port Augusta SA — Spencer Gulf coastal plain from on-site met mast
eis_specs.energy_yield_method: WAsP modelling with SA mesoscale wind resource; P50 net yield
eis_specs.connection_substation_name: Port Augusta North 275/66 kV substation (ElectraNet), near Port Augusta SA
eis_specs.connection_distance_note: ~3 km to ElectraNet Port Augusta North 275/66 kV substation. Port Augusta is a major SA electricity node.
eis_specs.connection_augmentation: Minimal augmentation. Port Augusta is a central node of the SA transmission network. PARP replaces former Alinta Energy coal/gas plant capacity at Port Augusta.
eis_specs.notes: 210 MW wind (50 x 4.2 MW Vestas V150) as part of the 280 MW wind + 100 MW solar PARP. Developer: TagEnergy (formerly RATCH Australia). EPC: Elecnor. SA EPA + EPBC approval 2018. Now operating (2022). Strategically replaces decommissioned Port Augusta coal plant capacity.
